mechanism_narrative: Rising retail sales signal sustained consumer demand growth, leading to positive earnings expectations and upward pressure on the S&P 500 over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 20-day forward returns are positive in fewer than 55% of months when RSAFS rises month-over-month, or walk-forward Rank IC falls below 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: A federal funds rate above 5% restricts credit availability and raises discount rates, causing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 posts positive 20-day returns in more than 50% of instances when FEDFUNDS exceeds 5.0%, or the signal Rank IC is not significantly negative (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous increases in nonfarm payrolls and decreases in the unemployment rate indicate robust labor market absorption, amplifying consumption and corporate revenue growth to drive positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: When PAYEMS increases and UNRATE decreases simultaneously, S&P 500 60-day forward returns are not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.05) over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: During periods when unemployment is below 5%, accelerating industrial production signals supply-side overheating and imminent monetary tightening, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
regime_hint: late-cycle expansion with tight labor markets
failure_condition: When UNRATE is below 5.0% and INDPRO rises, S&P 500 generates positive 20-day returns in more than 50% of instances, or the conditional Sharpe ratio is not negative.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month increases in CPIAUCSL reflect rising inflation pressure that compresses real earnings expectations and elevates discount rates, generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: During months when CPIAUCSL increases, S&P 500 5-day forward returns are not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.05) or the Rank IC exceeds -0.02.

mechanism_narrative: A decline in housing starts exceeding 5 percent over three months signals weakening construction activity and broader demand contraction, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: When HOUST declines more than 5% over 3 months, S&P 500 60-day forward returns are negative in fewer than 50% of triggered instances.

mechanism_narrative: An unemployment rate above 6 percent signals deteriorating household income and collapsing aggregate demand, driving negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: When UNRATE exceeds 6.0%, S&P 500 20-day forward returns are negative in fewer than 50% of triggered instances, or the mean return is not significantly below zero (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Positive growth in real GDP indicates broad-based economic expansion and rising corporate revenues, supporting positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: S&P 500 60-day forward returns following positive quarterly GDPC1 growth are not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.05) over any 3-year rolling window.
